commit.message:
Auto-reconnect to running scans when repo is selected

- Add change listener to repo-select dropdown
- When repo selected, automatically detect running experiments
- If scan in progress, auto-reconnect without confirmation prompt
- Reconnection streams output from running experiment via /scan endpoint
- Server lock file ensures we connect to existing scan, not start new one
- Improves UX on page refresh - users see running scan continue instantly

Flow:
  1. User refreshes page
  2. Selects repo from dropdown
  3. JavaScript detects running_experiment from API
  4. Auto-reconnects and shows live output
  5. No confirmation dialog needed

commit.message:
Fix OTA commit: SRAM-resident SD reads + sector-0-last write order

Root cause: sd_read_blocks was in flash (XIP) at offset 0x265b8.
During OTA commit, erasing sectors beyond 0x26000 destroyed the
SD read code mid-copy, producing a corrupted flash image.

Fix:
- Mark entire SD SPI read path as __no_inline_not_in_flash_func:
  spi_transfer, spi_read, spi_write, sd_cmd, sd_cmd_end,
  sd_wait_token, sd_read_block, sd_read_blocks
- New ota_commit_from_sd() in SRAM: writes sectors 1..N first,
  then sector 0 last (keeps boot2/vectors valid until final moment)
- Park Core 1 via volatile ota_halt_core1 flag (not multicore_lockout
  which conflicts with CYW43 WiFi driver)
- Reboot immediately after sector 0 write without restoring interrupts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(lobby): remove revalidatePath from startGame, add try/finally to handleStartGame

revalidatePath('/play') inside the startGame Server Action caused Next.js
to re-render the lobby page as part of the action response. The lobby saw
room.status === 'playing', returned null, and fired a server-side
redirect('/play') that raced with the host's direct router.push(), leaving
the spinner stuck indefinitely.

- Remove revalidatePath('/play') from startGame() — the /play landing page
  revalidates naturally on next visit; revalidateMultiplayerPaths() used by
  updateSettings and claimHost is unchanged
- Wrap handleStartGame() in try/catch/finally so setIsStarting(false) always
  runs; unexpected errors are surfaced via setStartError()

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: improve box-drawing character rendering in embedded terminal

Add @xterm/addon-webgl to replace the default canvas renderer with
WebGL-accelerated rendering. The canvas renderer draws each character
cell independently, producing visible gaps between box-drawing
characters (U+2500 range) at non-integer DPI values. The WebGL
renderer uses GPU texture atlas rendering that eliminates these
cell-gap artifacts, matching external terminal quality.

Falls back gracefully to the canvas renderer if WebGL is unavailable.

Add automatic `flyway` label for PRs touching Flyway migrations (#1280)

### **Behov / Bakgrunn**

PRs som endrer Flyway-migreringer under
`migreringer/src/main/resources/db/postgres/` ble ikke automatisk
merket, noe som gjorde det vanskelig å raskt identifisere
databaseendringer i PR-oversikten.

### **Løsning**

- Legger til `.github/labeler.yml` med glob-regel som trigges på
endringer i `migreringer/src/main/resources/db/postgres/**`
- Legger til `.github/workflows/labeler.yml` som kjører
`actions/labeler@v6` på alle PRs og setter `flyway`-labelen automatisk

Forutsetter at `flyway`-labelen er opprettet manuelt i repoet.

### **Andre endringer**

Ingen.

N/A

> ```yaml
> flyway:
>   - changed-files:
> - any-glob-to-any-file:
'migreringer/src/main/resources/db/postgres/**'
